searchIndex.title: Installation Requirements
searchIndex.description: Requires .NET Framework version 4.7.2 (included in Windows 10 & 11; Windows 7/8 users must install it separately).

searchIndex.title: Post-Installation Setup
searchIndex.description: Launch Crew Chief, click 'Download sound pack', click 'Download driver names', select your game from the top-right list, click 'Start Application', then launch your game.

searchIndex.title: Configuration Properties
searchIndex.description: Customise Crew Chief's behaviour via the 'Properties' button. Defaults are sensible. Search for game-specific preferences by typing: ACS, AMS, R3E, rF1, rF2 or pCars.

searchIndex.title: Swearing and Complaints
searchIndex.description: Swearing is disabled by default (enable via Properties UI). 'Max complaints per session' defaults to 60 to prevent excessive grumbling; set to 0 to eliminate complaints entirely.

searchIndex.title: Troubleshooting Startup Issues
searchIndex.description: If Crew Chief fails to start, delete configuration files at C:\Users\[username]\AppData\Local\Britton_IT_Ltd\ and delete all subfolders. (AppData is hidden; enable hidden folders in File Explorer).

searchIndex.title: Log Files and Crash Reports
searchIndex.description: Crew Chief logs information in the Console window. Logs are saved to <Documents>\CrewChiefV4\debugLogs with timestamps. Upload logs to the forum, Discord, or via confidential email for help.

searchIndex.title: Windows Speech Recognizer
searchIndex.description: Built-in Windows system. Requires better quality voice input but can be trained to recognise individual voices. Enable by checking 'Prefer Windows speech recogniser' in Properties. For non-English Windows, add the English (US) language pack.

searchIndex.title: Microsoft Speech Platform
searchIndex.description: Optimised for noisy environments and poor quality sound. No training required. Requires installing the Speech Platform Runtime (SpeechPlatformRuntime.msi) and the English language pack (MSSpeech_SR_en-US_TELE.msi or en-GB).

searchIndex.title: Voice Recognition Device and Modes
searchIndex.description: Uses the Windows 'Default' recording device (select in Windows Sounds). Two modes: 'Always On' (listens continuously) or 'Hold Button' (push-to-talk). nAudio is not recommended as it lacks trigger-word support.

searchIndex.title: Free Dictation Chat (Experimental)
searchIndex.description: Available for iRacing, pCars2, and R3E. Types chat messages via speech. Trigger word is 'chat'. Requires good accuracy; complete speech recognition training for best results.

searchIndex.title: Rally Voice Commands
searchIndex.description: Uses a significantly lower confidence threshold due to the many permutations of words in pace notes. 'Hold button' mode is recommended to prevent accidental stage-note entries from background noise.

searchIndex.title: Car Status Commands
searchIndex.description: Reports the status of the specified car component or performance metric.

searchIndex.title: Temperatures (Good/Bad) Commands
searchIndex.description: Gives a good/bad type response for the specified temperatures.

searchIndex.title: Temperatures (Actual Values) Commands
searchIndex.description: Gives the actual temperatures.

searchIndex.title: Race Information Commands
searchIndex.description: Reports the specified race information.

searchIndex.title: Message Toggle Commands
searchIndex.description: Switches off or switches messages back on.

searchIndex.title: Session Time / Laps Remaining
searchIndex.description: Reports the remaining time or laps in the session.

searchIndex.title: Spotter Toggle Commands
searchIndex.description: Switches the spotter on and off. Note: even in 'leave me alone' mode the spotter still operates unless explicitly switched off.

searchIndex.title: Penalty Status Commands
searchIndex.description: Reports the penalty status.
examples: do i still have a penalty

searchIndex.title: Mandatory Pit Stop Commands
searchIndex.description: Reports if a pit stop is required.
examples: do i have a mandatory pit stop
do i have a mandatory stop
do i have to make a pit stop

searchIndex.title: Opponent Info (by Driver Name) Commands
searchIndex.description: Reports information about a specific opponent driver (by last name).

searchIndex.title: Opponent Info (by Race Position) Commands
searchIndex.description: Reports information about the opponent in a specific race position.

searchIndex.title: Car Ahead / Behind Info Commands
searchIndex.description: Reports information about the car immediately ahead or behind.
examples: what's the car in front's last lap
what's the guy in front's last lap
what's the car ahead's last lap
what's the guy ahead's last lap
what's the car in front's best lap
what's the guy in front's best lap
what's the car ahead's best lap
what's the guy ahead's best lap
what's the car in front's irating
what's the guy in front's irating
what's the car in front's license class
what's the guy in front's license class
what's the car ahead's license class
what's the guy ahead's license class
what's the car behind's last lap
what's the guy behind's last lap
what's the car behind's best lap
what's the guy behind's best lap
what's the car behind's license class
what's the guy behind's license class

searchIndex.title: Sector Time Commands
searchIndex.description: Reports the player's sector times.

searchIndex.title: Leader and On-Track Position Commands
searchIndex.description: Reports the current leader or the name of the car ahead/behind.
examples: who's in front in the race

searchIndex.title: Gaps / Deltas Mode Commands
searchIndex.description: Switches on or off automatic gap/delta readouts on each lap.

searchIndex.title: Miscellaneous Commands
searchIndex.description: Various utility and information commands.
examples: what tyre type am i on
how much fuel do i need for 10 minutes
how much fuel do i need for 5 laps

searchIndex.title: RaceRoom Tyre Pace Comparison
searchIndex.description: RaceRoom only. Gives lap time deltas for the best lap on each tyre type used during the session.

searchIndex.title: Rolling / Formation Lap Commands
searchIndex.description: Enables or disables manual rolling-start mode (used by some online leagues).

searchIndex.title: Attack / Defend Strategy Commands
searchIndex.description: Reports where you are gaining or losing time relative to the car ahead/behind.

searchIndex.title: Corner Name Readout
searchIndex.description: Reads out each corner name when you hit the mid-point of the corner, for this lap only (useful for testing corner name mappings).

searchIndex.title: Damage and Full Status Commands
searchIndex.description: Reports damage, combined status (damage + temps + fuel), session status, or a full update combining all.

searchIndex.title: Fuel to Finish Commands
searchIndex.description: Reports how many litres or gallons Crew Chief thinks will be needed to finish the race.
examples: how much fuel to the end
how much fuel do we need

searchIndex.title: Car Class Information Commands
searchIndex.description: Reports whether the car ahead/behind is in your class, or what class it is.
examples: is the car ahead in my class
is the car ahead my class
is the car ahead the same class as me
is the car in front in my class
is the car in front my class
is the car in front the same class as me
is the car behind in my class
is the car behind my class
is the car behind the same class as me
what class is the car ahead
what class is the guy ahead
what class is the car in front
what class is the guy in front
what class is the car behind
what class is the guy behind

searchIndex.title: Pitstop Timing / Strategy Commands
searchIndex.description: Times the next pitstop (practice only) or estimates traffic and race positions on pit exit.
examples: where will i be after a stop?
what will happen if i pit?

searchIndex.title: Message Timing (Corners/Braking) Commands
searchIndex.description: Delays non-critical messages in challenging sections of the track, or disables the delay.
examples: don't talk in the braking zones
no messages in the braking zones

searchIndex.title: Alarm Clock Commands
searchIndex.description: Sets or clears alarms. Supports 12/24-hour format and multiple alarms.

searchIndex.title: Cut Track Warning Commands
searchIndex.description: Enables or disables cut track warnings.

searchIndex.title: Monitored Drivers Commands
searchIndex.description: Adds or removes drivers from the monitored list so Crew Chief keeps you informed about them.

searchIndex.title: iRacing Pitstop Configuration Commands
searchIndex.description: Configures pitstop options for iRacing (fuel, tyres, tearoff, fast repair, etc.).
examples: pitstop change left front tyre pressure 32
pitstop fuel to the end of the race

searchIndex.title: RaceRoom Pitstop Configuration Commands
searchIndex.description: Configures pitstop options for RaceRoom (tyre compound, aero, suspension, penalty, refuel, etc.).

searchIndex.title: Pitstop Plan Review Commands
searchIndex.description: Reports all selected actions for the next pitstop.

searchIndex.title: iRacing SOF and Incident Commands
searchIndex.description: Reports Strength of Field, incident count, and incident limit (iRacing).
examples: what is the strength of field
how many incidents do i have

searchIndex.title: Driver Ranking/Reputation Commands (if available)
searchIndex.description: Reports ranking, reputation, or rating of a specified driver.
examples: what's the car in front's ranking

searchIndex.title: rFactor 2 Multi-Function Display Commands
searchIndex.description: rFactor 2 specific. Selects the Multi-Function Display screen.
examples: select multi-function display screen display sectors
sector times pit menu tyres temps
